title: Restructure PHP File Naming and Routing Conventions
description:
Restructure the PHP file naming conventions for cleaner URLs and future extensibility:

1. Move `login.php` to root as `index.php` so no file extension is needed in the URL
2. Rename `Migration.php` to `hub.php` for a generic landing page
3. Rename the dictionary object-level page to `object.php`

Authentication redirects:
- Unauthenticated users accessing `select.php` or `migrate.php` are redirected to `index.php`
- Authenticated users accessing `index.php` are redirected to `select.php`
acceptance_criteria: login.php is moved to root as index.php
Dictionary object-level page is renamed to object.php
Unauthenticated users are redirected to index.php
Authenticated users on index.php are redirected to select.php
middleware_changes: Authentication redirect logic added: unauthenticated → index.php, authenticated → select.php. File renaming and routing updates.
implementation_notes: File renames: login.php → index.php (moved to root), Migration.php → hub.php, dictionary page → object.php. Redirect logic ensures proper routing based on auth state.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the file moves and redirect logic. QA was performed to verify all redirects work correctly.
